Q1 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A cantilever beam is subjected to a time-varying load given by F(t ) = 1000 sin(10t) N. The beam has a length of 200 mm. The ultimate tensile strength of material is Sut = 520 N/mm2. The fillet in the beam has a theoretical stress concentration factor kt = 1.3 and the notch sensitivity factor at the fillet is q = 0.85, then the required diameter of the beam to withstand 10000 cycles without
failure will be _____ mm.

NAT
Correct answer: 19.57
Solution

19.57 (19.11 - 20.01)

 
 
Q2 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A simply supported circular beam of length 800 mm is subjected to a fluctuating load varying from –100 N to 200 N at the center. The beam is made of cold drawn steel with ultimate tensile strength Sut = 600 N/mm2 and yield strength Syt = 350 N/mm2, the required factor of safety is 2. If ka = 0.7, kb = 0.85, kc = 0.7 and kd = 0.89, then the required diameter of beam using Goodman’s criterion for fatigue failure is

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

 
Q3 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A solid circular shaft, 15 mm diameter is subjected to torsional shear stress, which varies from 0 to 35 N/mm2 and at the same time, is subjected to an axial stress that varies from –15 to +30 N/mm2. The shaft is made of steel (Sut = 540 N/mm2 and Syt = 400 N/mm2) and corrected endurance limit of the shaft is 200 N/mm2. The frequency of variation of these stresses is equal to the shaft speed, the factor of safety using Goodman’s criterion is _____.

NAT
Correct answer: 4.05
Solution

4.05 (4.00 - 4.10)

 
Q4 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

According to the maximum strain theory, equivalent stress in a system subjected to σ, τ and  is given by

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

 
 
Q5 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A solid circular shaft of 40 mm diameter is loaded by bending moment 700 Nm, torque 1000 Nm and axial compressive force of 40 kN. The shaft material is ductile with yield strength of 300 MPa. The factor of safety according to Tresca theory of failure is

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

 
Q6 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A plate clutch consists of one pair of contacting surfaces. The inner and outer diameters of the friction disk are 120 mm and 240 mm respectively. The coefficient of friction is 0.2, and the permissible intensity of pressure is 2 N/mm2, the torque transmitting capacity of the clutch using uniform wear theory is ______ Nm.

NAT
Correct answer: 814.3
Solution

814.3 (813 - 815)

 
Q7 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

For a cone clutch, the semi-cone angle of the cone is 12.5° and the ratio of mean radius of the clutch to face width is 3. The normal intensity of the pressure is 0.2 N/mm2 and coefficient of friction between contact faces is 0.2. For uniform wear theory, cone clutch is transmitting 8 kW of power at 720 rpm, if internal radius is 140 mm, then outer diameter will be _____ mm. [Round off to two decimal places]

NAT
Correct answer: 289.18
Solution

289.18 (288.55 - 290.11)

 
Q8 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A simple block brake as shown in figure, provides a braking torque of 400 N-m. The diameter of the brake drum is 800 mm. The brake drum rotates at 120 rpm and coefficient of friction is 0.2, then the actuating force (F) will be ______ N. Assume drum rotates in anti-clockwise direction.

 
 
Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

 
 
Q9 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

For the simple band brake shown in figure, the drum diameter is 500 mm and the angle of lap of the band is 240°. If the coefficient of friction is 0.3 and braking force is 100 N, then the braking torque is

 
 
Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

 
Q10 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A centrifugal clutch consists of four shoes, each having a mass of 1 kg. In the engaged position, the radius to the centre of gravity of each shoe is 150 mm, while the inner radius of the drum is 200 mm. The coefficient of friction is 0.2. The preload in the spring is adjusted in such a way that the spring force at the beginning of engagement is 800 N, power transmitted by the clutch at
2400 rpm is ______ kW. (Round off to two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 348.84
Solution

348.84 (347 - 350)

 
 
Q11 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A stepper shaft transmit a torque varying from 600 N-m to 1200 N-m. The ultimate tensile strength of material of shaft is 600 MPa and yield stress of the material is 400 MPa. Correction for type of loading is 0.6, size factor is 0.85, and surface finish factor is 0.8, diameter of the shaft for an infinite life and factor of safety of 2 (Using Soderberg equation) is

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

 
Q12 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

The bending moment M applied to a solid shaft carries a maximum direct stress fy at elastic failure, what should be the value of the twisting moment T, acting alone on the same shaft, to produce same elastic failure.

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(b, c, d)

 
Q13 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A bolt is acted upon by an axial pull of 20 kN along with a transverse shear force of 10 kN, the diameter of the bolt required according to maximum distortion energy theory is. [Take elastic limit of the bolt material, 200 MPa and factor of safety 2]

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

 
Q14 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The shaft of an overhang crank is subjected to a force P of 2 kN as shown in figure. The shaft is made of plain carbon steel of tensile yield strength 400 N/mm2 and factor of safety is 2. The diameter of the shaft by maximum shear stress theory is

 
 
Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

 
Q15 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A 25 mm diameter shaft subjected to reverse bending moment is made of forged steel having Sut = 600 N/mm2. There is a step in the shaft and the theoretical stress concentration factor at the step is 2.1. The notch sensitivity factor is 0.84. The endurance limit of the shaft is ______ N/mm2.
(Assume other stress concentration constants to be 1)

NAT
Correct answer: 155.925
Solution

155.925 (155 - 157)

 
 
Q16 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The ratio of inner radius to the outer radius for maximum torque transmission in a disc clutch will be, using uniform wear theory

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

 
Q17 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements are correct?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(b, c, d)
• Ductile material undergoes local yielding and distribute the concentrated stress when subjected to stress concentration.
• However, when the load is fluctuating, the stress at discontinuities may exceed the endurance limit, and fail by fatigue.
• The effect of stress concentration is more severe in case of brittle material, due to the their
inability of plastic deformation.
